fix_summary:
All 881 tests pass. The fix was simple: `NUMBERVALUE` was only stripping regular space characters (`" "`), but the input `"  \t  "` contains tabs. After removing only spaces, `"\t"` remained and caused `strconv.ParseFloat` to fail with `#VALUE!`. 

The fix uses `strings.Map` with `unicode.IsSpace` to strip all whitespace characters (spaces, tabs, newlines, etc.), so `"  \t  "` correctly becomes `""`, which then returns `0` per Excel's spec: *"If an empty string ("") is specified as the Text argu...
